title: The Hollow Fortification
description: Walking the parapet line behind Mifflin's fire grates, you find the earthworks entirely empty for three hundred yards in every direction—no sentries, no reserves, no second line. The deception is thinner than Washington's orders implied: Mifflin's unit numbers fewer than two hundred men maintaining the illusion of an occupied garrison of thousands. If the British send a patrol probe rather than waiting for daylight, the hollow will be discovered immediately. Tallmadge's manifest tally confirms that the last interior regiment filed off to the slip forty minutes ago.
